perturbation.original_text: 4.  Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.  Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This agreement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordance with principles of fairness and equity. With respect to any issues, disputes, lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder, the parties agree to attempt mediation before seeking resolution in any other forum.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the governing law and mandatory jurisdiction clause creates a legal ambiguity and contradicts the PRC Contract Law, which mandates that contracts involving foreign parties must specify applicable laws.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that contracts involving foreign parties must explicitly state which country's laws apply to the contract. By removing the clause specifying that the laws of the People's Republic of China govern the agreement, the modified text directly contradicts this legal requirement, creating legal uncertainty and potential unenforceability issues.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Decision of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress on Setting up the National Defense Education Day of the Whole People [Effective] 全国人民代表大会常务委员会关于设立全民国防教育日的决定 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Standing Committee of the National People's Congress Date issued： 08-31-2001 Effective date: 08-31-2001 Level of Authority: Decisions on Legal Issues Area of Law: Military Decision of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress on Setting up the National Defense Education Day of the Whole People 全国人民代表大会常务委员会关于设立全民国防教育日的决定 (Adopted at the 23rd Meeting of the Standing Committee of the Ninth National People's Congress on August 31, 2001) （２００１年８月３１日第九届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第二十三次会议通过） In order to make the national defense education universal and strengthen the whole people's sense of national defense, and in accordance with Article 12 of Law of the People's Republic of China on National Defense Education , it is decided at the 23rd Meeting of the Standing Committee of the Ninth National People's Congress that: “The third Saturday in September of each year shall be the National Defense Education Day of the Whole People.” 为普及国防教育，增强全民的国防观念，根据《 中华人民共和国国防教育法 》第 十二条 的规定，第九届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第二十三次会议决定：每年９月的第三个星期六为全民国防教育日。 【法宝引证码】 北大法宝 www.lawinfochina.com Message: Please kindly comment on the present translation. Contract Law of the People's Republic of China was a law in the PRC that came into force on October 1, 1999, and was abolished on Jan 1, 2021 with the enforcement of Civil Code of the People's Republic of China . It is the main source of contract law in the PRC. The most recent version combines many parts of Chinese contract law that were previously parts of other substantive laws, and has led to increased legal uniformity. The law includes both general and specific provisions and also has the legal rules that relate to the formation, fulfilment and breach of contracts. The law allows for various different types of contracts that can be entered into. [ 1 ] "The principles of the freedom of contract, of good faith, and of the fostering of transactions have informed and guided the formulation of the Contract Law and are embodied in many of its major provisions." [ 2 ] The main purpose of the law is stated in Article 1: "This Law is enacted for the purpose of protecting the legitimate rights and interests of the parties to contracts, maintaining the socio-economic order and promoting the socialist modernization." [ 3 ] Article 2 has the definition for a contract: "For the purpose of this Law, a contract means an agreement on the establis
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the explicit governing law of the People's Republic of China, replacing it with 'principles of fairness and equity' and introducing a mediation step before other resolution methods. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation both highlight that Article 126 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China requires contracts involving foreign parties to explicitly state which country's laws apply. Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to the 'Contract Law of the People's Republic of China' as 'the main source of contract law in the PRC.' The original text adheres to this by specifying PRC law, while the changed text omits it, creating a clear contradiction by removing a legally mandated requirement for contracts involving foreign parties.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS: a) Entrusting party ( Party A) and Recipient ( Party B ) Propose to conduct discussions and negotiations regarding the cooperative project. Exchanging of relevant business and technical information is required for the ongoing business discussions or cooperation between Entrusting party and Recipient with respect to ( Project name) _ ___, this agreement is entered into by and between Entrusting party and Recipient through friendly consultations and under the principle of mutual benefit and joint development. b) In the course of discussing, evaluating and negotiating the proposed agreement, Entrusting party and Recipient have agreed to enter into confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS: a) Entrusting party ( Party A) and Recipient ( Party B ) Propose to conduct discussions and negotiations regarding the cooperative project. b) In the course of discussing, evaluating and negotiating the proposed agreement, Entrusting party and Recipient have agreed to enter into confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the section that specifies that exchanging business and technical information is required undermines the foundation of the NDA. This may contradict Article 4 of the Trade Secret Protection Law of China, which requires reasonable measures to protect trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Trade Secret Protection Law of China, Article 4
perturbation.law_citation: Article 4 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019) (formerly known as Trade Secret Protection Law)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019), also known as the Trade Secret Protection Law, requires owners of trade secrets to adopt measures to protect their confidentiality. By removing the statement that the NDA is designed to facilitate the exchange of relevant business and technical information, and replacing it with a vague and nonspecific confidentiality agreement, the modified text undermines the purpose of the NDA and makes it more difficult for the owner of trade secrets to demonstrate that reasonable measures were taken to protect the confidentiality of their trade secrets, potentially leading to a violation of Article 4.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the necessity of exchanging business and technical information, which is directly related to the purpose of the NDA. The changed text removes this explicit statement, weakening the demonstration of reasonable measures taken to protect trade secrets, as suggested in the Law Explanation. While the provided scraped snippets are irrelevant, the Law Explanation states that this could potentially lead to a violation of Article 4 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019). The Omission Legal Contradiction definition states that a legal contradiction exists when a contract removes essential information, creating legal loopholes, which is what happened here.

perturbation.original_text: 1.  Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization. Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refers to data and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms. Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party or otherwise make use of the confidential information. Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party or otherwise make use of the confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement that confidential information have a 'clear label or designation of Confidential Information' contradicts Article 9 of China's Anti-Unfair Competition Law, which implies that the company must take measures to identify trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019)
perturbation.law_citation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law requires reasonable confidentiality measures to be taken. Removing the requirement for clear labeling of confidential information weakens protection, potentially violating this law.

perturbation.justification: The original text requires "clear label or designation of 'Confidential information'," which is removed in the changed text. The Perturbation Explanation states this contradicts Article 9 of China's Anti-Unfair Competition Law because it implies that companies must take measures to identify trade secrets. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that removing clear labeling weakens protection and potentially violates this law. While the scraped snippets are irrelevant, the removal of a clear labeling requirement directly undermines the ability to identify and protect confidential information, which creates a legal contradiction based on the provided reasoning.

perturbation.original_text: Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Nevertheless, the party possessing the confidential information may keep duplicates of the documents or materials described above.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the reference to Article 4 (Confidentiality Obligation) and the purpose enshrined within it removes a crucial safeguard, potentially undermining the protection of trade secrets. This removal of purpose might conflict with requirements for reasonable measures to protect confidential information, as outlined in Article 9 of China's Anti-Unfair Competition Law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019)
perturbation.law_citation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China (Amended 2019)
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the limitation on the purpose for retaining copies, the company opens itself up to potential claims of misuse of confidential information. This could weaken its position in defending against claims of trade secret misappropriation under Article 9, which emphasizes the need for reasonable measures to protect confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the use of confidential information copies to the purpose defined in Article 4, thereby protecting trade secrets. The changed text removes this limitation, potentially allowing misuse of the confidential information. This omission contradicts the need for reasonable measures to protect confidential information, as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation, which are derived from Article 9 of China's Anti-Unfair Competition Law. While no law snippets are present, the provided explanations detail a potential violation of trade secret protection due to the removal of purpose limitation.
